Subclasses ( Length, Mass, Time, Temperature, …) represent specific dimensions and carry their own typed arithmetic operator overloads. Internally every quantity is stored in canonical SI base units, with the original display unit retained for formatting and for the left-operand-wins rule on arithmetic results (see specification §14.4). Returns double The numeric value in the target unit. Exceptions UnknownUnitException If unit is not registered. DimensionMismatchException If unit has a different dimension. Throws if the dimensions differ. public int CompareTo(EngineeringUnit? other) Parameters other EngineeringUnit Returns int Equals(EngineeringUnit?) Use Equals(EngineeringUnit?, double) for tolerance comparison. public bool Equals(EngineeringUnit? other) Parameters other EngineeringUnit Returns bool Equals(EngineeringUnit?, double) True if both operands are the same dimension and their canonical values agree within relativeTolerance. public bool Equals(EngineeringUnit? other, double relativeTolerance) Parameters other EngineeringUnit relativeTolerance double Returns bool Equals(object?) public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object Returns bool GetHashCode() public override int GetHashCode() Returns int Pow(double) Raises this quantity to a non-integer power. Use the integer overload when applicable for cleaner semantics. public EngineeringUnit Pow(double exponent) Parameters exponent double Returns EngineeringUnit Pow(int) Raises this quantity to an integer power. The result's dimension is this dimension multiplied element-wise by the exponent; the runtime resolves to the matching subclass (e.g. Length.Pow(2) → Area, Length.Pow(3) → Volume) or to DerivedUnit if no subclass matches. public EngineeringUnit Pow(int exponent) Parameters exponent int Returns EngineeringUnit ToDualString() Returns a two-system display like "0.500 m (1.640 ft)". api/EngineeringUnits.MixtureMolarConvers.summary: Class MixtureMolarConversions Namespace EngineeringUnits Assembly EngineeringUnits.dll Bridges molar-basis quantities (e.g. MolarEnergy in J/mol) to mass-basis quantities (e.g. SpecificEnergy in J/kg) and back, using a mixture's MolarMass. public static class MixtureMolarConversions Inheritance object MixtureMolarConversions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Pure dimensional analysis can't perform this conversion: molar mass is a mixture property, not a unit. So this bridge takes the MW explicitly, keeping the call sites self-documenting: var H_molar = new MolarEnergy(1164.7, "J/mol"); var MW = new MolarMass(20.5433, "g/mol"); var H_mass = MixtureMolarConversions.ToMassBasis(H_molar, MW); // → J/kg All conversions go through SI canonical units internally (kg/mol for MW, J/mol for energy, etc.), so the user can supply any input unit and request any output unit. Methods ToMassBasis(MolarDensity, MolarMass) Convert molar density (mol/m³) to mass density (kg/m³): ρ_mass = ρ_molar * MW. public static Density ToMassBasis(MolarDensity molar, MolarMass mw) Parameters molar MolarDensity mw MolarMass Returns Density ToMassBasis(MolarEnergy, MolarMass) Convert molar energy (J/mol) to specific (mass-basis) energy (J/kg): e_specific = e_molar / MW. public static SpecificEnergy ToMassBasis(MolarEnergy molar, MolarMass mw) Parameters molar MolarEnergy mw MolarMass Returns SpecificEnergy ToMassBasis(MolarHeatCapacity, MolarMass) Convert molar heat capacity (J/(mol·K)) to specific heat capacity (J/(kg·K)): cp_specific = cp_molar / MW. public static SpecificHeatCapacity ToMassBasis(MolarHeatCapacity molar, MolarMass mw) Parameters molar MolarHeatCapacity mw MolarMass Returns SpecificHeatCapacity ToMolarBasis(Density, MolarMass) Convert mass density (kg/m³) to molar density (mol/m³): ρ_molar = ρ_mass / MW. public static MolarDensity ToMolarBasis(Density mass, MolarMass mw) Parameters mass Density mw MolarMass Returns MolarDensity ToMolarBasis(SpecificEnergy, MolarMass) Convert specific energy (J/kg) to molar energy (J/mol): e_molar = e_specific * MW. public static MolarEnergy ToMolarBasis(SpecificEnergy mass, MolarMass mw) Parameters mass SpecificEnergy mw MolarMass Returns MolarEnergy ToMolarBasis(SpecificHeatCapacity, MolarMass) Convert specific heat capacity (J/(kg·K)) to molar heat capacity (J/(mol·K)): cp_molar = cp_specific * MW. public static MolarHeatCapacity ToMolarBasis(SpecificHeatCapacity mass, MolarMass mw) Parameters mass SpecificHeatCapacity mw MolarMass Returns MolarHeatCapacity

api/EngineeringUnits.Temperature.html.summary: Class Temperature Namespace EngineeringUnits Assembly EngineeringUnits.dll An absolute temperature. SI base unit: kelvin. public sealed class Temperature : EngineeringUnit, IFormattable, IComparable<EngineeringUnit>, IEquatable<EngineeringUnit> Inheritance object EngineeringUnit Temperature Implements IFormattable IComparable<EngineeringUnit> IEquatable<EngineeringUnit> Inherited Members EngineeringUnit.CanonicalValue EngineeringUnit.DisplayUnit EngineeringUnit.Dimension EngineeringUnit.Precision EngineeringUnit.Value EngineeringUnit.As(string) EngineeringUnit.ToString() EngineeringUnit.ToString(string) EngineeringUnit.ToString(string, IFormatProvider) EngineeringUnit.ToDualString() EngineeringUnit.Equals(EngineeringUnit) EngineeringUnit.Equals(EngineeringUnit, double) EngineeringUnit.CompareTo(EngineeringUnit) EngineeringUnit.Pow(int) EngineeringUnit.Pow(double) EngineeringUnit.Abs() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Supported units include K, °C / degC, °F / degF, and °R / degR. Note that non-Kelvin temperature units use affine (scale + offset) conversion rather than purely multiplicative scaling. Arithmetic on absolute temperatures is intentionally restricted in v1. Adding two temperatures is physically meaningless and throws. Subtraction produces a result in kelvin since the difference of two temperatures is a delta, and a proper TemperatureDelta type is deferred to v1.x (specification §14.9). For now, callers needing temperature arithmetic should reason in kelvin via As(string). api/EngineeringUnits.UnitPreferences.htm.summary: Class UnitPreferences Namespace EngineeringUnits Assembly EngineeringUnits.dll Per-dimension display-unit preferences plus notation style. Used by the "P" format code and by ToDualString(). public sealed class UnitPreferences Inheritance object UnitPreferences Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Preferences are a per-dimension map (Decision 14.17). There is no binary SI-vs-US toggle because even within SI the conventionally-preferred unit depends on the dimension and the application — meter for length, kilogram for mass, but kilometer for travel and millimeter for machining. api/EngineeringUnits.html.summary: Namespace EngineeringUnits Classes Acceleration An acceleration quantity. SI base unit: meter per second squared (m/s²). AmountOfSubstance An amount of substance. SI base unit: mole. AngularVelocity An angular velocity quantity. SI base unit: radian per second (rad/s). Area An area quantity. SI base unit: square meter (m²). AreaDensity Area density (mass per unit area). SI base unit: kilogram per square-meter (kg/m²). Density A mass density quantity (mass per unit volume). SI base unit: kg/m³. DerivedUnit A quantity whose dimension does not match any registered subclass. Returned by arithmetic when the result combination has no dedicated type, e.g. Mass * Length / Time^3. DimensionMismatchException Thrown when an operation requires two quantities of the same dimension but they differ (e.g. adding a Length to a Time, or calling As("ft") on a Mass). DimensionlessQuantity A quantity with no physical dimension — a pure number. Produced by arithmetic that reduces to all-zero dimensions (e.g. velocity / velocity, length / length). DynamicViscosity Dynamic (absolute) viscosity. SI base unit: pascal-second (Pa·s). ElectricCapacitance Electric capacitance. SI unit: farad (F = C/V). ElectricCharge Electric charge. SI unit: coulomb (C = A·s). ElectricCurrent Electric current. SI base unit: ampere (A). ElectricResistance Electric resistance. SI unit: ohm (Ω = V/A). ElectricalConductance Electrical conductance (reciprocal of resistance). SI unit: siemens (S = 1/Ω). Energy An energy / work quantity. SI base unit: joule (J). EnergyDensity Energy density (energy per unit volume). SI base unit: joule per cubic-meter (J/m³). EnergyPerArea Energy per unit area (e.g. fracture toughness E/A, surface energy). SI base unit: joule per square meter (J/m²). Dimensionally equal to SurfaceTension (M/T²) but semantically distinct — kept separate for the same reason Torque and Energy are separate despite sharing M·L²/T². EngineeringUnit Abstract base class for all physical-quantity types. Carries a value expressed in canonical SI base units, a display unit, and significant-figure metadata. EngineeringUnitException Base class for all exceptions thrown by this library. Force A force quantity. SI base unit: newton (N). Frequency A frequency quantity. SI base unit: hertz (Hz = 1/s). HeatCapacity Heat capacity (per object, not per mass). SI base unit: joule per kelvin (J/K). HeatFluxDensity Heat flux density (power per unit area). SI base unit: watt per square-meter (W/m²). Inductance Electrical inductance. SI unit: henry (H = V·s/A = Wb/A). KinematicViscosity Kinematic viscosity (dynamic viscosity divided by density). SI base unit: square-meter per second (m²/s). Length A length quantity. SI base unit: meter. Mass A mass quantity. SI base unit: kilogram. MassFlowRate A mass flow rate (mass per unit time). SI base unit: kg/s. MixtureMolarConversions Bridges molar-basis quantities (e.g. MolarEnergy in J/mol) to mass-basis quantities (e.g. SpecificEnergy in J/kg) and back, using a mixture's MolarMass. MolarDensity A molar density quantity. SI canonical: mol/m³ (dimension N/L³). MolarEnergy A molar energy quantity. SI canonical unit: J/mol (dimension L²·M/(T²·N)). MolarHeatCapacity A molar heat-capacity (or molar entropy) quantity. SI canonical: J/(mol·K). MolarMass A molar mass quantity. SI canonical unit: kg/mol (dimension M/N). Momentum Linear momentum (mass × velocity). SI base unit: kilogram-meter per second (kg·m/s). PlaneAngle A plane angle quantity. SI base unit: radian (rad). Power A power quantity. SI base unit: watt (W). Pressure A pressure quantity. SI base unit: pascal (Pa). SpecificEnergy Specific energy (energy per unit mass). SI base unit: joule per kilogram (J/kg). SpecificHeatCapacity Specific heat capacity (per unit mass). SI base unit: joule per kilogram-kelvin (J/(kg·K)). SpecificVolume Specific volume (volume per unit mass — reciprocal of density). SI base unit: cubic-meter per kilogram (m³/kg). SurfaceTension Surface tension (force per unit length). SI base unit: newton per meter (N/m). Temperature An absolute temperature. SI base unit: kelvin. TemperatureDelta A temperature difference (delta). Distinct from Temperature because temperature differences obey linear-only conversion (no offset), while absolute temperatures use affine conversion. ThermalConductivity Thermal conductivity. SI base unit: watt per meter-kelvin (W/(m·K)). Time A time-duration quantity. SI base unit: second. Torque A torque (moment of force) quantity. SI base unit: newton-meter (N·m). UnitCatalog The library's catalog of recognized units. Populated once at module load and then frozen — concurrent reads are safe without locking. UnitParseException Thrown when a value+unit string cannot be parsed. UnitPreferences Per-dimension display-unit preferences plus notation style. Used by the "P" format code and by ToDualString(). UnknownUnitException Thrown when a unit symbol is not registered in the catalog. Velocity A velocity (linear speed) quantity. SI base unit: meter per second (m/s). Voltage Electrical potential difference (voltage). SI unit: volt (V = W/A = J/C). Volume A volume quantity. SI base unit: cubic meter (m³). VolumetricFlowRate A volumetric flow rate (volume per unit time). SI base unit: m³/s. Structs DimensionSignature The physical dimension of a quantity, expressed as a signed exponent for each SI base dimension plus a pseudo-dimension for plane angle (radians). Unit A unit of measurement: a name, a dimension, and conversion factors to/from the canonical SI-base value. Enums NotationStyle Notation style for compound units in formatted output.

MMSCFD defaults to GPSA / AGA Report No. 3 standard conditions (14.73 psia, 60 °F), the convention used on US interstate gas pipelines. Variants: Symbol Conditions Notes MMSCFD 14.73 psia, 60 °F Default. GPSA / AGA. MMSCFD_petro 14.696 psia, 60 °F Petroleum / refining. MMSCFD_iupac 100 kPa, 0 °C IUPAC standard. The volumetric conversion is the same for all three (geometric ft³ → m³); the variants differ only in the conditions they carry. How to register additional units Out of scope for v1 — the catalog is frozen at module load to make multithreaded reads lock-free. articles/design.html.title: Design Overview | EngineeringUnits API Documentation
articles/design.html.summary: Design Overview Class hierarchy EngineeringUnit (abstract base) ├── DimensionlessQuantity — pure numbers, optional Reynolds/Mach/etc. tag ├── Length, Mass, Time, Temperature — 4 of 7 SI base dimensions ├── ElectricCurrent, PlaneAngle — base + pseudo-base ├── TemperatureDelta — differences, linear-only conversion ├── Area, Volume — geometric ├── Velocity, Acceleration, AngularVelocity, Frequency — kinematic ├── Force, Pressure, Energy, Torque, Power — dynamic ├── Density, Momentum, MassFlowRate, VolumetricFlowRate — flow ├── DynamicViscosity, KinematicViscosity — viscosity ├── ThermalConductivity, HeatCapacity, HeatFluxDensity — thermal ├── SpecificHeatCapacity, SpecificEnergy — per-mass ├── SpecificVolume, AreaDensity, EnergyDensity, SurfaceTension ├── Voltage, ElectricCharge, ElectricResistance — electrical └── ElectricalConductance, ElectricCapacitance, Inductance DerivedUnit — fallback for arbitrary dimension combinations Internal representation Every quantity stores: Canonical value — magnitude in SI base units (a double). Display unit — the Unit struct the user provided or last set as preferred. Precision — significant figures for display (byte, default 4). Dimension signature — 8-slot [L, M, T, I, Θ, N, J, A] of sbyte exponents. The canonical SI representation makes equality, comparison, and arithmetic trivial. Display unit preserves the left-operand-wins convention without adding state. Left-operand-wins for mixed units When two quantities of the same dimension but different units are added or subtracted, the result takes the left operand's display unit: new Length(5, "ft") + new Length(6, "in") // 5.5 ft new Length(6, "in") + new Length(5, "ft") // 66 in The choice is predictable and matches pint/boost::units. Multiplication produces a derived unit via dimension addition; division via subtraction. Torque vs Energy disambiguation Both Torque and Energy have the fundamental dimension M·L²·T⁻². They're distinct subclasses chosen by operand order: new Force(10, "N") * new Length(2, "m") // Torque (force-first: N·m, lbf·ft) new Length(2, "m") * new Force(10, "N") // Energy (length-first: J, ft·lbf) Explicit conversions (AsTorque(), AsEnergy()) reinterpret without changing the canonical value when the user knows what they meant. Parsing follows the same convention: "5 ft*lbf" → Energy, "5 lbf*ft" → Torque. Angle as a 9th pseudo-dimension Radians are dimensionless in standard SI but are tracked as a separate slot here so: Frequency (Hz = 1/s, no angle) and AngularVelocity (rad/s, has angle) stay distinguishable Linear power (Force × Velocity, no angle) and Rotational power (Torque × AngularVelocity, has angle) — both watts numerically, but separable through the angle slot The angle slot has no effect on simple kinematic quantities — radians cancel out in standard derivations. Its purpose is to prevent accidental conflation when an operation should yield a different quantity. Industry-specific units Some units don't fit the SI prefix pattern cleanly. The catalog registers them as first-class entries: MMSCFD defaults to interstate-pipeline conditions (14.73 psia / 60 °F per GPSA / AGA Report No. 3). Named alternates MMSCFD_petro (14.696 psia / 60 °F) and MMSCFD_iupac (100 kPa / 0 °C) cover other industries. bbl (petroleum barrel = 42 US gallons), MMBTU, cP, cSt, Da. Open-core licensing The core EngineeringUnits library is MIT-licensed. Specific integration assemblies (ExcelDNA UDFs, ASP.NET Core helpers) live in separate repositories and may carry their own licenses while consuming this library as a NuGet dependency. The MIT license explicitly permits closed downstream consumers.

articles/getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | EngineeringUnits API Documentation
articles/getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started Install dotnet add package EngineeringUnits First quantity using EngineeringUnits; var l = new Length(5, "ft"); Console.WriteLine(l); // "5.000 ft" Console.WriteLine(l.As("m")); // 1.524 Console.WriteLine(l.In("m")); // "1.524 m" Mixed-unit arithmetic var total = new Length(5, "ft") + new Length(6, "in"); Console.WriteLine(total); // "5.500 ft" — left operand's unit governs Cross-type arithmetic The compiler picks the right result type from operand types: var area = new Length(3, "m") * new Length(4, "m"); // Area var v = new Length(100, "m") / new Time(10, "s"); // Velocity var F = new Mass(10, "kg") * new Acceleration(9.81, "m/s^2"); // Force (N) var P = F / new Area(0.1, "m^2"); // Pressure (Pa) var V = new Voltage(120, "V"); var I = V / new ElectricResistance(60, "Ω"); // ElectricCurrent (Ohm's law) var pwr = V * I; // Power (W) Industry-specific units var flow = new VolumetricFlowRate(10, "MMSCFD"); // 14.73 psia / 60 °F default var visc = new DynamicViscosity(1.0, "cP"); // water at 20 °C var energy = new Energy(1, "MMBTU"); Format codes var dia = new Length(0.5, "m"); Console.WriteLine(dia.ToString("L")); // "0.5000 meter" Console.WriteLine(dia.ToString("U")); // "1.640 ft" (US-customary) Console.WriteLine(dia.ToString("D")); // "0.5000 m (1.640 ft)" (dual) Console.WriteLine(dia.ToString("N6")); // "0.500000 m" (force 6 sig figs) Console.WriteLine(dia.ToString("{cm}")); // "50.00 cm" (force unit) Preferred unit profiles UnitPreferences.Default = UnitPreferences.OilAndGas; Console.WriteLine(new Pressure(1, "atm").ToString("P")); // displays in psia Console.WriteLine(new Volume(1, "m^3").ToString("P")); // displays in bbl Built-in profiles: SIScientific, SIEveryday, UsCustomary, UsEngineering, OilAndGas, Machining. Temperature arithmetic Adding two absolute temperatures throws (physically meaningless). Subtraction produces a TemperatureDelta: var t1 = new Temperature(30, "degC"); var t2 = new Temperature(20, "degC"); var dt = t1 - t2; // TemperatureDelta of 10 degC var t3 = t1 + new TemperatureDelta(5, "degC"); // Temperature of 35 degC Pow / Abs var l = new Length(3, "m"); var a = l.Pow(2); // Area (9 m²) var v = l.Pow(3); // Volume (27 m³) var l2 = new Area(9, "m^2").Pow(0.5); // back to Length (3 m) var negL = new Length(-5, "ft"); var absL = negL.Abs(); // Length (5 ft) — preserves display unit

index.html.title: EngineeringUnits | EngineeringUnits API Documentation
index.html.summary: EngineeringUnits A .NET class library for representing physical quantities as first-class typed values, with units carried explicitly and preserved through arithmetic. Quick links Getting Started — install, first quantity, first cross-type computation Design Overview — class hierarchy, internal SI representation, left-operand-wins, Torque vs Energy disambiguation Catalog Reference — every registered quantity type and unit API Reference — auto-generated from XML doc comments Specification — full design document Status Version 0.1.0-alpha. Phases 1–5 implemented plus Tiers A–D cleanup. 283 tests passing. The library is consumable as a NuGet package (EngineeringUnits) targeting .NET Standard 2.0, callable from both .NET Framework 4.7.2+ and modern .NET 6+. License MIT — see LICENSE.
